disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A longstanding subcutaneous lump on the upper back enlarged over 48 hours then spontaneously ruptured about 3 hours ago
trigger_question_examples: What happened with the lump, and how long has it been there?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had this lump on my upper back for months and was told it was harmless. Over the last couple of days it swelled up and got sore, then about three hours ago it just burst on its own.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent bleeding and purulent (yellowish) discharge from the ruptured lesion
trigger_question_examples: Is it bleeding or leaking anything?
What is coming out of it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It keeps bleeding a bit and starting up again, and there's thick yellowish stuff coming out of it too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Localised redness and severe tenderness around the lesion (patient-reported warmth), but no systemic fever, rigors or confusion
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin around it red, hot or spreading?
Have you had any fevers, shivering or shaking chills?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The skin around it is red and really sore and feels warm, but I haven't had any fevers or shakes and I don't feel confused — just exhausted from the pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cannot reach or dress the lesion independently (posterior upper back, lives alone); bleeding staining bedsheets
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to clean and dress it yourself?
Is anyone with you who can help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — it's on my upper back where I can't reach, and I live on my own so there's nobody here to help me clean or cover it. It's bled onto my bedsheets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe pain preventing sleep and preventing lying supine
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's severe — I can't lie on my back at all and it's kept me awake all night.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion has recently changed rapidly (growth, bleeding, crusting) — a feature that must not be dismissed remotely and mandates in-person assessment and histopathology
trigger_question_examples: Has the lump changed in size, colour or shape recently?
Has it started bleeding or crusting where it never did before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it grew quite quickly over the last two days and now it's bleeding and crusting, which it's never done before.
